commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3099055ce6ee027c2c5844a8e3f0d718ac8660d8)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds moderator-driven exact skill-version revocation across Convex schema/API, artifact delivery guards, security-verdict responses, `clawhub-mod`, tests, schema package outputs, specs, and changelog.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR, but source inspection is enough to verify the review findings: the candidate tree changes the API/CLI contract while `docs/http-api.md` and the moderator README remain stale.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor and maintainer follow-up is needed for real behavior proof, public/operator docs, and release-note cleanup; automation should not take this because the proof must come from a real setup.

target.title: feat: add exact skill version revocation
target.body:
## Summary

- What changed: adds moderator-controlled exact skill-version revocation for ClawHub skills, blocks public artifact-byte delivery for revoked versions, and exposes exact revocation state through the existing bulk security-verdict endpoint.
- Why: clients need a machine-readable way to stop installing a specific compromised artifact without hiding unrelated versions of the same skill.

## Linked Issue

- Related: companion OpenClaw consumer PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90201.

## Screenshots

N/A

- [x] Screenshots/recordings attached, or `N/A`

## Behavioural Proof

Backend/API proof:

- `POST /api/v1/skills/{slug}/versions/{version}/revocation` records exact `active`/`revoked` state with an audit reason.
- Revoked exact versions return `410 Version not available` for ZIP, raw-file, Skill Card, export, and package compatibility raw-file paths.
- `POST /api/v1/skills/-/security-verdicts` returns `decision: "fail"`, `reasons: ["version.revoked"]`, and `revocation: { revoked: true, revokedAt }` for revoked exact versions.

- [x] Behavioural proof included, or `N/A`

## Security / Trust Impact

- [x] Security/trust impact explained

Security impact: moderators can revoke one exact skill version while keeping historical evidence and non-revoked versions available. Install clients get an explicit exact-version revocation signal; lookup failures remain non-signals.

## Data / Deploy Impact

- [x] Data/deploy impact explained

Data/deploy impact: adds optional `skillVersions.manualRevocation` metadata. Existing rows remain compatible; public API response changes are additive.

## Verification

- [x] `bun run ci:static`
- [x] Focused tests for touched behavior: `bunx vitest run convex/downloads.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts convex/skills.versionRevocation.test.ts packages/clawhub-mod/src/commands/moderation.test.ts`
- [x] `bun run ci:unit`
- [x] Broader gate when required (`ci:types-build`, `ci:packages`, `ci:e2e-http`): `bun run ci:pr`
- [x] Other: `bun run --cwd packages/schema build`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub-mod typecheck`, `git diff --check`
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski
